name: continental_philosophy
assumptions: Philosophy must engage with historical, cultural, and political contexts
The subject and experience are central to philosophical inquiry
Language, power, and social structures shape what counts as knowledge
Philosophical reflection includes critique of its own methods and assumptions

norms.message: Should engage with phenomenological method or existential themes
Should situate arguments in historical and cultural context
Should include self-reflexive critique of methodological assumptions
Should acknowledge hermeneutic dimensions of understanding

bridge_warnings.explanation: Heideggerian Dasein is being-in-the-world through equipmental totality; computational models of cognition reduce this to information processing. Treating technological mediation as mere tool-use ignores the ontological stakes.

Continental critique (Foucault, Feenberg) shows that technological design embeds power relations. Treating software architecture as value-neutral engineering ignores the political philosophy of technology.
